# CRD Lifecycle: Cross-Repo Architectural Analysis

## Files Examined

### apimachinery (Foundation Layer)
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/scheme.go` — Core `Scheme` struct: bidirectional mapping between Go types and GroupVersionKind (GVK); foundation for all type registration
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/types.go` — `TypeMeta` struct (APIVersion+Kind fields); `RawExtension` for raw embedded objects
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema/group_version.go` — `GroupVersionKind`, `GroupVersionResource`, `GroupResource`, `GroupVersion` types; all API address space navigation
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/types.go` — `ObjectMeta` (name, namespace, UID, labels, annotations, etc.) and `TypeMeta` (apiVersion, kind); embedded in every API object
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/meta.go` — `ObjectMetaAccessor` interface; accessor methods on ObjectMeta
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/unstructured/unstructured.go` — `Unstructured` struct backed by `map[string]interface{}`; implements `runtime.Object`, `metav1.Object`, and `metav1.ListInterface`; the universal carrier for custom resources

### apiextensions-apiserver (CRD Type Definitions + Server-Side Lifecycle)
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apis/apiextensions/types.go` — Internal hub type `CustomResourceDefinition` (embeds `metav1.TypeMeta`) and `CustomResourceDefinitionSpec` with group, names, scope, validation, conversion, versions fields
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apis/apiextensions/register.go` — Registers hub types with `runtime.Scheme` under `apiextensions.k8s.io/__internal`; defines `SchemeGroupVersion`, `SchemeBuilder`, `AddToScheme`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apis/apiextensions/v1/types.go` — External v1 `CustomResourceDefinition` (embeds both `metav1.TypeMeta` and `metav1.ObjectMeta`); the type users apply to the cluster
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apis/apiextensions/v1beta1/types.go` — Deprecated v1beta1 external type; preserved for backwards compatibility
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apis/apiextensions/install/install.go` — Calls `AddToScheme` for internal, v1, v1beta1 types; sets v1 > v1beta1 priority in Scheme
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apiserver/apiserver.go` — Initialises the apiextensions apiserver; calls `install.Install(Scheme)`, registers unversioned types; wires CRD REST storage, controllers, and informer factory
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apiserver/validation/validation.go` — `SchemaValidator` / `SchemaCreateValidator` interfaces; OpenAPI-based structural schema validation using `kube-openapi/pkg/validation`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/registry/customresourcedefinition/etcd.go` — `REST` struct wrapping `genericregistry.Store`; etcd-backed storage for `CustomResourceDefinition` objects; adds cleanup finalizer on delete
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/registry/customresourcedefinition/strategy.go` — Strategy (PrepareForCreate, PrepareForUpdate, Validate, Canonicalize) for CRD objects
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/registry/customresource/etcd.go` — `CustomResourceStorage` with REST, StatusREST, ScaleREST; stores CRs as `unstructured.Unstructured`; scale subresource reads/writes replicas into unstructured fields
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/registry/customresource/strategy.go` — Strategy for custom resource instances; structural schema enforcement, pruning, defaulting, CEL validation
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apiserver/customresource_handler.go` — `crdHandler` HTTP handler; dynamically routes `/apis/<group>/<version>/<resource>` to per-CRD `customResource` REST storage; holds `crdLister` to discover active CRDs; uses `Unstructured` throughout

### apiextensions-apiserver (Client-Side — generated)
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/client/clientset/clientset/clientset.go` — Generated `Clientset` implementing typed interface for `ApiextensionsV1` and `ApiextensionsV1beta1`; backed by `client-go/rest`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/client/informers/externalversions/apiextensions/v1/customresourcedefinition.go` — Generated `CustomResourceDefinitionInformer`; creates `cache.SharedIndexInformer` with List/Watch from typed clientset; exposes `Lister()`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/client/listers/apiextensions/v1/customresourcedefinition.go` — Generated `CustomResourceDefinitionLister`; reads from `cache.Indexer` (in-memory, populated by informer); returns typed `*v1.CustomResourceDefinition`

### client-go (Dynamic Client + Generic Informer Infrastructure)
- `staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/dynamic/interface.go` — `Interface` (Resource → NamespaceableResourceInterface) and `ResourceInterface` (CRUD, Watch, Patch, Apply); all methods operate on `*unstructured.Unstructured`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/dynamic/simple.go` — `DynamicClient` implementation backed by `rest.Interface`; takes `schema.GroupVersionResource` to build HTTP paths; all responses decoded as Unstructured
- `staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/dynamic/dynamicinformer/informer.go` — `dynamicSharedInformerFactory`; `ForResource(gvr)` creates generic informers keyed by GVR; uses `dynamic.Interface` + `dynamiclister` for GVR-addressed cache
- `staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/dynamic/dynamiclister/lister.go` — `ResourceLister` / `NamespaceLister` over `cache.Indexer`; returns `*unstructured.Unstructured`

### api (Built-in Type Patterns + Scale Dependency)
- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/autoscaling/v1/` — `Scale` type used by `customresource/etcd.go` ScaleREST; demonstrates the pattern where CRD scale subresources project into a standard autoscaling type
- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/`, `apps/v1/`, etc. — Define the canonical pattern: internal hub type → external versioned type → Scheme registration → REST storage; CRDs follow the exact same pattern, but dynamically

---

## Dependency Chain

1. **Foundation — apimachinery**
   - `schema.GroupVersionKind` / `GroupVersionResource` — the universal address space for all Kubernetes types
   - `runtime.Scheme` — bidirectional registry mapping Go types ↔ GVKs; central to encoding/decoding
   - `metav1.TypeMeta` + `metav1.ObjectMeta` — embedded in every persisted API object
   - `unstructured.Unstructured` — `map[string]interface{}` representation that can hold any API object without a registered Go struct

2. **Type Definitions — apiextensions-apiserver**
   - Internal hub type (`apiextensions.CustomResourceDefinition`) registered with `SchemeGroupVersion = apiextensions.k8s.io/__internal`
   - External v1 type (`v1.CustomResourceDefinition`) registered with `apiextensions.k8s.io/v1`; embeds `metav1.TypeMeta` + `metav1.ObjectMeta`
   - `install.Install(scheme)` registers all versions and sets priority: v1 > v1beta1

3. **Server-Side — validation → etcd storage → HTTP handler**
   - **Validation**: `apiserver/validation.SchemaValidator` validates CR instances against the CRD's OpenAPI schema before admission
   - **CRD etcd storage** (`registry/customresourcedefinition/etcd.go`): Generic store backed by etcd via `genericregistry.Store`; persists `CustomResourceDefinition` meta-objects; adds cleanup finalizer on delete
   - **CR etcd storage** (`registry/customresource/etcd.go`): Separate generic store for actual custom resource instances; `NewFunc` returns `&unstructured.Unstructured{}` pre-tagged with GVK; `ScaleREST` projects replica paths into `autoscaling/v1.Scale`
   - **HTTP handler** (`apiserver/customresource_handler.go`): `crdHandler` HTTP filter dynamically builds and caches per-CRD `customResourceStorage` when a CRD becomes established; routes `/apis/<group>/<version>/<resource>` requests; reads CRD state via `listers.CustomResourceDefinitionLister`

4. **Client-Side — typed client → informer → lister → dynamic client**
   - **Typed clientset** (`client/clientset/clientset/clientset.go`): Generated code; `ApiextensionsV1()` gives access to `CustomResourceDefinitions()` CRUD methods; uses `client-go/rest` under the hood
   - **SharedIndexInformer** (`client/informers/.../customresourcedefinition.go`): Uses the typed clientset's List/Watch to populate an in-memory `cache.Indexer`; shared across all consumers to minimise API server load
   - **Lister** (`client/listers/apiextensions/v1/customresourcedefinition.go`): Read-only view over the informer's `cache.Indexer`; `Get(name)` returns `*v1.CustomResourceDefinition` without an API server round-trip
   - **DynamicClient** (`client-go/dynamic/simple.go`): GVR-addressed client that sends/receives raw JSON; all objects as `*unstructured.Unstructured`; used by controllers that manage arbitrary CRs without compiled-in type knowledge
   - **Dynamic informer** (`client-go/dynamic/dynamicinformer/`): Like typed informers but keyed by `schema.GroupVersionResource`; enables watching arbitrary CRs generically

---

## Analysis

### Cross-Project Dependency Flow

```
apimachinery
  ├── runtime.Scheme         ←── used by ALL other sub-projects
  ├── schema.GVK / GVR       ←── universal type address
  ├── metav1.TypeMeta/ObjectMeta ←── embedded in every API object
  └── unstructured.Unstructured  ←── dynamic CR representation
         ↓
apiextensions-apiserver
  ├── types.go (hub + v1)    ←── CRD Go structs, embed apimachinery types
  ├── register.go / install  ←── Scheme registration
  ├── validation             ←── structural schema, CEL, OpenAPI validation
  ├── registry/crd/etcd.go   ←── CRD meta-object persistence in etcd
  ├── registry/cr/etcd.go    ←── CR instance persistence via Unstructured
  └── apiserver/handler.go   ←── dynamic HTTP routing, uses listers
         ↓
client-go
  ├── dynamic.Interface      ←── GVR-addressed, Unstructured-based client
  ├── dynamic/informer       ←── generic informer for any GVR
  └── tools/cache            ←── SharedIndexInformer, Indexer (shared infra)
         ↑
apiextensions-apiserver (generated clients)
  ├── clientset              ←── typed client wrapping client-go/rest
  ├── informers (generated)  ←── typed SharedIndexInformer
  └── listers (generated)    ←── typed read from Indexer
```

### How CRD Types Bridge the Four Sub-Projects

The `CustomResourceDefinition` object itself follows the universal Kubernetes object pattern established by `apimachinery`: it embeds `metav1.TypeMeta` and `metav1.ObjectMeta` from `apimachinery`, is registered in `runtime.Scheme` (also from `apimachinery`), and is persisted through `genericregistry.Store`. This means a CRD object is handled identically to a Pod or Deployment at the storage level—the only difference is its *effect*: a CRD object's existence causes the apiextensions-apiserver to dynamically provision a new REST endpoint for the custom resource kind it describes.

The custom resource *instances* (CR data) live entirely as `unstructured.Unstructured` throughout their server-side lifecycle. There are no compiled-in Go structs for user-defined CRs. The `crdHandler` HTTP layer reads the CRD spec (via lister → informer → typed clientset), constructs a schema validator and storage, and routes all CRUD operations through `customresource/etcd.go`, which stores and retrieves raw JSON documents as `Unstructured` maps.

### The Role of Scheme and GVK in Type Registration

`runtime.Scheme` is the central registry. When `install.Install(scheme)` is called at apiserver startup, it registers three versions of `CustomResourceDefinition`:
- The internal hub type under `apiextensions.k8s.io/__internal`
- The v1 external type under `apiextensions.k8s.io/v1`
- The v1beta1 external type under `apiextensions.k8s.io/v1beta1`

The Scheme's `gvkToType` and `typeToGVK` maps enable the codec factory to decode incoming API requests (regardless of version) into the hub type for validation and storage, and to encode outgoing responses into the client's preferred version. Version priority (`v1 > v1beta1`) is also stored in the Scheme and governs which version the API server advertises as preferred.

For *custom resource instances*, no Scheme registration occurs (there are no Go structs). Instead, the `crdHandler` constructs a runtime codec that directly serializes/deserializes JSON into `Unstructured` maps, bypassing the Scheme for the data plane.

### How Unstructured Enables Dynamic Custom Resource Access

`unstructured.Unstructured` implements both `runtime.Object` (so it can flow through the generic apiserver machinery) and `metav1.Object` (so standard metadata accessors like `GetName()`, `GetUID()`, `GetLabels()` work correctly). Its backing store is a plain `map[string]interface{}`, which can represent any JSON document without needing a compiled Go type.

The `DynamicClient` in `client-go/dynamic` exploits this: it takes a `schema.GroupVersionResource` at runtime, constructs the URL path, and returns `*unstructured.Unstructured` for all operations. This makes it the primary API for controllers that manage arbitrary CRs (e.g., operator frameworks, GitOps controllers). The `dynamicSharedInformerFactory` builds on top of this, providing a watch-based cache keyed by GVR.

### The Checkpoint Between Server-Side Storage and Client-Side Informer Caching

The critical checkpoint is the **List/Watch API**. When a `CustomResourceDefinitionInformer` starts, it calls:
1. `client.ApiextensionsV1().CustomResourceDefinitions().List(...)` — initial sync, populates the local `cache.Indexer`
2. `client.ApiextensionsV1().CustomResourceDefinitions().Watch(...)` — ongoing watch stream, incrementally updates the Indexer

The `crdHandler` server-side also uses a `CustomResourceDefinitionLister` (backed by its own informer) to read established CRDs without round-tripping to etcd on every request. This means there are two informer caches: one inside the apiextensions-apiserver process (server-side, for routing), and one in any external controller process (client-side, for reconciliation). Both subscribe to the same etcd-backed watch stream.

---

## Summary

A Custom Resource Definition flows through the Kubernetes ecosystem in two distinct planes: the **CRD meta-object plane** (the CRD itself, persisted as a typed Go struct through `genericregistry.Store` backed by etcd) and the **CR instance plane** (actual custom resource data, handled entirely as `unstructured.Unstructured` with no compiled-in Go types). `apimachinery` provides the universal vocabulary—`Scheme`, `GVK/GVR`, `TypeMeta/ObjectMeta`, and `Unstructured`—that all four sub-projects share; `apiextensions-apiserver` implements the CRD type definitions, validation, storage, and dynamic HTTP routing; and `client-go` provides both the generated typed clients/informers/listers for the CRD meta-objects and the generic `DynamicClient` + `dynamicSharedInformerFactory` that let controllers manage arbitrary custom resource instances by GVR at runtime.

`runtime.Scheme` is the central registry. When `install.Install(scheme)` is called at apiserver startup:
- The internal hub type is registered under `apiextensions.k8s.io/__internal`
- The v1 external type under `apiextensions.k8s.io/v1`
- The v1beta1 external type under `apiextensions.k8s.io/v1beta1`
- Version priority is set: v1 > v1beta1

The Scheme's `gvkToType` and `typeToGVK` maps enable the codec factory to decode incoming API requests (regardless of version) into the hub type for validation and storage, and to encode outgoing responses into the client's requested version.

For *custom resource instances*, no Scheme registration occurs. Instead, `crdHandler` builds a runtime codec that directly serializes/deserializes JSON into `Unstructured` maps, bypassing the Scheme for the CR data plane. The GVK is carried inside the Unstructured's `Object["apiVersion"]` and `Object["kind"]` fields.

### How Unstructured Enables Dynamic Custom Resource Access

`unstructured.Unstructured` implements both `runtime.Object` (allowing it to flow through the generic apiserver machinery unchanged) and `metav1.Object` (so standard metadata accessors like `GetName()`, `GetUID()`, `GetLabels()` work correctly via nested map traversal). Its backing store is a `map[string]interface{}` that can represent any JSON document.

The `DynamicClient` exploits this: it takes a `schema.GroupVersionResource` at runtime, constructs the URL path, serializes/deserializes JSON, and returns `*unstructured.Unstructured`. This makes it the primary API for controllers that manage arbitrary CRs without advance knowledge of the schema. The `dynamicSharedInformerFactory` builds on top, providing a watch-based cache keyed by GVR so controllers can avoid repeated API server calls.

### The Checkpoint Between Server-Side Storage and Client-Side Informer Caching

The critical bridge is the **List/Watch API endpoint**. When a `CustomResourceDefinitionInformer` starts, it executes:
1. `client.ApiextensionsV1().CustomResourceDefinitions().List(...)` — performs an initial bulk sync, populating the local `cache.Indexer` with all current CRDs
2. `client.ApiextensionsV1().CustomResourceDefinitions().Watch(...)` — subscribes to an etcd watch stream; incremental Add/Update/Delete events keep the Indexer current

The `crdHandler` server-side also holds its own `CustomResourceDefinitionLister` (backed by an internal informer) to look up established CRDs on every request without hitting etcd directly. This means there are two independent informer caches: one inside the apiextensions-apiserver process (for HTTP routing decisions) and one in any external controller process (for reconciliation logic). Both subscribe to the same underlying etcd watch stream through the API server's watch-cache layer.

---

## Summary

A Custom Resource Definition flows through the Kubernetes ecosystem across two planes: the **CRD meta-object plane**, where the CRD itself is a typed Go struct persisted through `genericregistry.Store` into etcd and accessed via generated clientsets, informers, and listers; and the **CR instance plane**, where actual custom resource data is handled entirely as `unstructured.Unstructured` with no compiled-in Go types, routed dynamically by `crdHandler` using GVK/GVR from apimachinery's schema package. `apimachinery` provides the universal vocabulary—`Scheme`, `GVK/GVR`, `TypeMeta/ObjectMeta`, and `Unstructured`—that all four sub-projects depend on; `apiextensions-apiserver` implements the CRD type definitions, validation pipeline, dual etcd storage layers (one for CRD meta-objects, one for CR instances), and dynamic HTTP routing; and `client-go` supplies both the generated typed clients/informers/listers for managing CRD objects and the generic `DynamicClient`/`dynamicSharedInformerFactory` that let controllers watch and reconcile arbitrary custom resource instances by GVR at runtime.

# big-code-cross-k8s-arch-001: Kubernetes CRD Lifecycle (Cross-Repo)

## Task

Trace how a Custom Resource Definition (CRD) flows through the Kubernetes ecosystem: from CRD type definitions and validation, through etcd storage and the dynamic HTTP handler, to the generated client, informers, listers, and the dynamic client. Map the complete lifecycle across four logical sub-projects within the Kubernetes monorepo.

## Context

- **Repository**: github.com/sg-evals/kubernetes--v1.30.0 (mirror of kubernetes/kubernetes) (Go, ~2.5M LOC)
- **Category**: Architectural Understanding (cross-repo)
- **Difficulty**: hard
- **Subsystem Focus**: staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/, staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/, staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/, staging/src/k8s.io/api/

## Architecture Overview

Kubernetes uses a staging directory structure where four logically separate projects coexist in one monorepo:

1. **apiextensions-apiserver** — CRD type definitions, validation, etcd storage, dynamic HTTP handler
2. **client-go** — Generated typed clients, informers, listers, and the dynamic client
3. **apimachinery** — Runtime scheme, GVK/GVR types, ObjectMeta, Unstructured representation
4. **api** — Internal hub types and group registration

## Requirements

1. Trace the CRD type definition layer: internal hub types, external v1 types, and Scheme registration
2. Map the server-side lifecycle: validation, etcd storage, and the dynamic customresource_handler
3. Trace the client-side access layer: typed clientset, SharedIndexInformer, Lister, and DynamicClient
4. Identify the foundational types in apimachinery that everything depends on: Scheme, GVK/GVR, TypeMeta/ObjectMeta, Unstructured

## CRD Lifecycle Summary

### Four Sub-Projects and Their Roles

| Sub-project | Role |
|---|---|
| **apimachinery** | Universal vocabulary: `runtime.Scheme`, `schema.GVK/GVR`, `metav1.TypeMeta/ObjectMeta`, `unstructured.Unstructured` |
| **apiextensions-apiserver** | CRD type definitions (hub + v1 + v1beta1), validation, etcd storage (CRD meta + CR instances), dynamic HTTP handler |
| **client-go** | `DynamicClient` (GVR-addressed, Unstructured-based), `dynamicSharedInformerFactory`, `cache.SharedIndexInformer` infrastructure |
| **api** | Standard API group patterns (e.g., `autoscaling/v1.Scale` used by CRD scale subresource); demonstrates hub→versioned→registration lifecycle that CRDs follow |

### Key Dependency Chain

1. **Foundation**: `apimachinery` — `runtime.Scheme` maps Go types ↔ GVKs; `Unstructured` = `map[string]interface{}` carrying any JSON without a compiled struct
2. **Type defs**: `apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apis/apiextensions/` — internal hub type + v1/v1beta1 external types all registered via `install.Install(scheme)`
3. **Server-side**:
   - Validation: `apiserver/validation` — OpenAPI/CEL structural schema check
   - CRD storage: `registry/customresourcedefinition/etcd.go` — persists the CRD meta-object itself
   - CR storage: `registry/customresource/etcd.go` — stores CR instances as `Unstructured`
   - HTTP routing: `apiserver/customresource_handler.go` — `crdHandler` dynamically provisions per-CRD REST storage, reads CRDs via `CustomResourceDefinitionLister`
4. **Client-side**:
   - Generated typed clientset → `SharedIndexInformer` → `CustomResourceDefinitionLister` (for the CRD meta-object)
   - `DynamicClient` → `dynamicSharedInformerFactory` (for arbitrary CR instances by GVR)

### Critical Insight

There are **two distinct storage/client stacks**: the CRD object itself is handled as a typed Go struct (same as any built-in resource), while the actual custom resource *instances* are handled entirely as `Unstructured` maps with no Scheme registration — the GVK rides inside the JSON, and the `crdHandler` reads the CRD spec at request time to validate and route accordingly.

31: // Scheme defines methods for serializing and deserializing API objects, a type
32: // registry for converting group, version, and kind information to and from Go
33: // schemas, and mappings between Go schemas of different versions. A scheme is the
34: // foundation for a versioned API and versioned configuration over time.
35: //
36: // In a Scheme, a Type is a particular Go struct, a Version is a point-in-time
37: // identifier for a particular representation of that Type (typically backwards
38: // compatible), a Kind is the unique name for that Type within the Version, and a
39: // Group identifies a set of Versions, Kinds, and Types that evolve over time. An
40: // Unversioned Type is one that is not yet formally bound to a type and is promised
41: // to be backwards compatible (effectively a "v1" of a Type that does not expect
42: // to break in the future).
43: //
44: // Schemes are not expected to change at runtime and are only threadsafe after
45: // registration is complete.
46: type Scheme struct {
47: 	// gvkToType allows one to figure out the go type of an object with
48: 	// the given version and name.
49: 	gvkToType map[schema.GroupVersionKind]reflect.Type
50: 
51: 	// typeToGVK allows one to find metadata for a given go object.
52: 	// The reflect.Type we index by should *not* be a pointer.
53: 	typeToGVK map[reflect.Type][]schema.GroupVersionKind
54: 
55: 	// unversionedTypes are transformed without conversion in ConvertToVersion.
56: 	unversionedTypes map[reflect.Type]schema.GroupVersionKind
57: 
58: 	// unversionedKinds are the names of kinds that can be created in the context of any group
59: 	// or version
60: 	// TODO: resolve the status of unversioned types.
61: 	unversionedKinds map[string]reflect.Type
62: 
63: 	// Map from version and resource to the corresponding func to convert
64: 	// resource field labels in that version to internal version.
65: 	fieldLabelConversionFuncs map[schema.GroupVersionKind]FieldLabelConversionFunc
66: 
67: 	// defaulterFuncs is a map to funcs to be called with an object to provide defaulting
68: 	// the provided object must be a pointer.
69: 	defaulterFuncs map[reflect.Type]func(interface{})
70: 
71: 	// converter stores all registered conversion functions. It also has
72: 	// default converting behavior.
73: 	converter *conversion.Converter
74: 
75: 	// versionPriority is a map of groups to ordered lists of versions for those groups indicating the
76: 	// default priorities of these versions as registered in the scheme
77: 	versionPriority map[string][]string
78: 
79: 	// observedVersions keeps track of the order we've seen versions during type registration
80: 	observedVersions []schema.GroupVersion

27: // Install registers the API group and adds types to a scheme
28: func Install(scheme *runtime.Scheme) {
29: 	utilruntime.Must(apiextensions.AddToScheme(scheme))
30: 	utilruntime.Must(v1beta1.AddToScheme(scheme))
31: 	utilruntime.Must(v1.AddToScheme(scheme))
32: 	utilruntime.Must(scheme.SetVersionPriority(v1.SchemeGroupVersion, v1beta1.SchemeGroupVersion))
33: }

51: // RawExtension is used to hold extensions in external versions.
52: //
53: // To use this, make a field which has RawExtension as its type in your external, versioned
54: // struct, and Object in your internal struct. You also need to register your
55: // various plugin types.
56: //
57: // // Internal package:
58: //
59: //	type MyAPIObject struct {
60: //		runtime.TypeMeta `json:",inline"`
61: //		MyPlugin runtime.Object `json:"myPlugin"`
62: //	}
63: //
64: //	type PluginA struct {
65: //		AOption string `json:"aOption"`
66: //	}
67: //
68: // // External package:
69: //
70: //	type MyAPIObject struct {
71: //		runtime.TypeMeta `json:",inline"`
72: //		MyPlugin runtime.RawExtension `json:"myPlugin"`
73: //	}
74: //
75: //	type PluginA struct {
76: //		AOption string `json:"aOption"`
77: //	}
78: //
79: // // On the wire, the JSON will look something like this:
80: //
81: //	{
82: //		"kind":"MyAPIObject",
83: //		"apiVersion":"v1",
84: //		"myPlugin": {
85: //			"kind":"PluginA",
86: //			"aOption":"foo",
87: //		},
88: //	}
89: //
90: // So what happens? Decode first uses json or yaml to unmarshal the serialized data into
91: // your external MyAPIObject. That causes the raw JSON to be stored, but not unpacked.
92: // The next step is to copy (using pkg/conversion) into the internal struct. The runtime
93: // package's DefaultScheme has conversion functions installed which will unpack the
94: // JSON stored in RawExtension, turning it into the correct object type, and storing it
95: // in the Object. (TODO: In the case where the object is of an unknown type, a
96: // runtime.Unknown object will be created and stored.)
97: //

99: // +protobuf=true
100: // +k8s:openapi-gen=true

## Files Examined

### apimachinery (Foundation Layer)

- `staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema/group_version.go` — `GroupVersionKind`, `GroupVersionResource`, `GroupResource`, `GroupVersion` types; all API address space navigation
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/types.go` — `ObjectMeta` (name, namespace, UID, labels, annotations, etc.) and `TypeMeta` (apiVersion, kind); embedded in every API object
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/meta.go` — `ObjectMetaAccessor` interface; accessor methods on ObjectMeta
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/unstructured/unstructured.go` — `Unstructured` struct backed by `map[string]interface{}`; implements `runtime.Object`, `metav1.Object`, and `metav1.ListInterface`; the universal carrier for custom resources

### apiextensions-apiserver (CRD Type Definitions + Server-Side Lifecycle)
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apis/apiextensions/types.go` — Internal hub type `CustomResourceDefinition` (embeds `metav1.TypeMeta`) and `CustomResourceDefinitionSpec` with group, names, scope, validation, conversion, versions fields
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apis/apiextensions/register.go` — Registers hub types with `runtime.Scheme` under `apiextensions.k8s.io/__internal`; defines `SchemeGroupVersion`, `SchemeBuilder`, `AddToScheme`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apis/apiextensions/v1/types.go` — External v1 `CustomResourceDefinition` (embeds both `metav1.TypeMeta` and `metav1.ObjectMeta`); the type users apply to the cluster
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apis/apiextensions/v1beta1/types.go` — Deprecated v1beta1 external type; preserved for backwards compatibility
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apis/apiextensions/install/install.go` — Calls `AddToScheme` for internal, v1, v1beta1 types; sets v1 > v1beta1 priority in Scheme
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apiserver/apiserver.go` — Initialises the apiextensions apiserver; calls `install.Install(Scheme)`, registers unversioned types; wires CRD REST storage, controllers, and informer factory
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apiserver/validation/validation.go` — `SchemaValidator` / `SchemaCreateValidator` interfaces; OpenAPI-based structural schema validation using `kube-openapi/pkg/validation`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/registry/customresourcedefinition/etcd.go` — `REST` struct wrapping `genericregistry.Store`; etcd-backed storage for `CustomResourceDefinition` objects; adds cleanup finalizer on delete
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/registry/customresourcedefinition/strategy.go` — Strategy (PrepareForCreate, PrepareForUpdate, Validate, Canonicalize) for CRD objects
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/registry/customresource/etcd.go` — `CustomResourceStorage` with REST, StatusREST, ScaleREST; stores CRs as `unstructured.Unstructured`; scale subresource reads/writes replicas into unstructured fields
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/registry/customresource/strategy.go` — Strategy for custom resource instances; structural schema enforcement, pruning, defaulting, CEL validation
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/apiserver/customresource_handler.go` — `crdHandler` HTTP handler; dynamically routes `/apis/<group>/<version>/<resource>` to per-CRD `customResource` REST storage; holds `crdLister` to discover active CRDs; uses `Unstructured` throughout

### apiextensions-apiserver (Client-Side — generated)
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/client/clientset/clientset/clientset.go` — Generated `Clientset` implementing typed interface for `ApiextensionsV1` and `ApiextensionsV1beta1`; backed by `client-go/rest`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/client/informers/externalversions/apiextensions/v1/customresourcedefinition.go` — Generated `CustomResourceDefinitionInformer`; creates `cache.SharedIndexInformer` with List/Watch from typed clientset; exposes `Lister()`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/apiextensions-apiserver/pkg/client/listers/apiextensions/v1/customresourcedefinition.go` — Generated `CustomResourceDefinitionLister`; reads from `cache.Indexer` (in-memory, populated by informer); returns typed `*v1.CustomResourceDefinition`

### client-go (Dynamic Client + Generic Informer Infrastructure)
- `staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/dynamic/interface.go` — `Interface` (Resource → NamespaceableResourceInterface) and `ResourceInterface` (CRUD, Watch, Patch, Apply); all methods operate on `*unstructured.Unstructured`
- `staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/dynamic/simple.go` — `DynamicClient` implementation backed by `rest.Interface`; takes `schema.GroupVersionResource` to build HTTP paths; all responses decoded as Unstructured
- `staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/dynamic/dynamicinformer/informer.go` — `dynamicSharedInformerFactory`; `ForResource(gvr)` creates generic informers keyed by GVR; uses `dynamic.Interface` + `dynamiclister` for GVR-addressed cache
- `staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/dynamic/dynamiclister/lister.go` — `ResourceLister` / `NamespaceLister` over `cache.Indexer`; returns `*unstructured.Unstructured`

### api (Built-in Type Patterns + Scale Dependency)
- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/autoscaling/v1/` — `Scale` type used by `customresource/etcd.go` ScaleREST; demonstrates the pattern where CRD scale subresources project into a standard autoscaling type
- `staging/src/k8s.io/api/core/v1/`, `apps/v1/`, etc. — Define the canonical pattern: internal hub type → external versioned type → Scheme registration → REST storage; CRDs follow the exact same pattern, but dynamically

---

## Dependency Chain

1. **Foundation — apimachinery**
   - `schema.GroupVersionKind` / `GroupVersionResource` — the universal address space for all Kubernetes types
   - `runtime.Scheme` — bidirectional registry mapping Go types ↔ GVKs; central to encoding/decoding
   - `metav1.TypeMeta` + `metav1.ObjectMeta` — embedded in every persisted API object
   - `unstructured.Unstructured` — `map[string]interface{}` representation that can hold any API object without a registered Go struct

2. **Type Definitions — apiextensions-apiserver**
   - Internal hub type (`apiextensions.CustomResourceDefinition`) registered with `SchemeGroupVersion = apiextensions.k8s.io/__internal`
   - External v1 type (`v1.CustomResourceDefinition`) registered with `apiextensions.k8s.io/v1`; embeds `metav1.TypeMeta` + `metav1.ObjectMeta`
   - `install.Install(scheme)` registers all versions and sets priority: v1 > v1beta1

3. **Server-Side — validation → etcd storage → HTTP handler**
   - **Validation**: `apiserver/validation.SchemaValidator` validates CR instances against the CRD's OpenAPI schema before admission
   - **CRD etcd storage** (`registry/customresourcedefinition/etcd.go`): Generic store backed by etcd via `genericregistry.Store`; persists `CustomResourceDefinition` meta-objects; adds cleanup finalizer on delete
   - **CR etcd storage** (`registry/customresource/etcd.go`): Separate generic store for actual custom resource instances; `NewFunc` returns `&unstructured.Unstructured{}` pre-tagged with GVK; `ScaleREST` projects replica paths into `autoscaling/v1.Scale`
   - **HTTP handler** (`apiserver/customresource_handler.go`): `crdHandler` HTTP filter dynamically builds and caches per-CRD `customResourceStorage` when a CRD becomes established; routes `/apis/<group>/<version>/<resource>` requests; reads CRD state via `listers.CustomResourceDefinitionLister`

4. **Client-Side — typed client → informer → lister → dynamic client**
   - **Typed clientset** (`client/clientset/clientset/clientset.go`): Generated code; `ApiextensionsV1()` gives access to `CustomResourceDefinitions()` CRUD methods; uses `client-go/rest` under the hood
   - **SharedIndexInformer** (`client/informers/.../customresourcedefinition.go`): Uses the typed clientset's List/Watch to populate an in-memory `cache.Indexer`; shared across all consumers to minimise API server load
   - **Lister** (`client/listers/apiextensions/v1/customresourcedefinition.go`): Read-only view over the informer's `cache.Indexer`; `Get(name)` returns `*v1.CustomResourceDefinition` without an API server round-trip
   - **DynamicClient** (`client-go/dynamic/simple.go`): GVR-addressed client that sends/receives raw JSON; all objects as `*unstructured.Unstructured`; used by controllers that manage arbitrary CRs without compiled-in type knowledge
   - **Dynamic informer** (`client-go/dynamic/dynamicinformer/`): Like typed informers but keyed by `schema.GroupVersionResource`; enables watching arbitrary CRs generically

---

## Analysis

### Cross-Project Dependency Flow

```
apimachinery
  ├── runtime.Scheme         ←── used by ALL other sub-projects
  ├── schema.GVK / GVR       ←── universal type address
  ├── metav1.TypeMeta/ObjectMeta ←── embedded in every API object
  └── unstructured.Unstructured  ←── dynamic CR representation
         ↓
apiextensions-apiserver
  ├── types.go (hub + v1)    ←── CRD Go structs, embed apimachinery types
  ├── register.go / install  ←── Scheme registration
  ├── validation             ←── structural schema, CEL, OpenAPI validation
  ├── registry/crd/etcd.go   ←── CRD meta-object persistence in etcd
  ├── registry/cr/etcd.go    ←── CR instance persistence via Unstructured
  └── apiserver/handler.go   ←── dynamic HTTP routing, uses listers
         ↓
client-go
  ├── dynamic.Interface      ←── GVR-addressed, Unstructured-based client
  ├── dynamic/informer       ←── generic informer for any GVR
  └── tools/cache            ←── SharedIndexInformer, Indexer (shared infra)
         ↑
apiextensions-apiserver (generated clients)
  ├── clientset              ←── typed client wrapping client-go/rest
  ├── informers (generated)  ←── typed SharedIndexInformer
  └── listers (generated)    ←── typed read from Indexer
```

### How CRD Types Bridge the Four Sub-Projects

The `CustomResourceDefinition` object itself follows the universal Kubernetes object pattern established by `apimachinery`: it embeds `metav1.TypeMeta` and `metav1.ObjectMeta` from `apimachinery`, is registered in `runtime.Scheme` (also from `apimachinery`), and is persisted through `genericregistry.Store`. This means a CRD object is handled identically to a Pod or Deployment at the storage level—the only difference is its *effect*: a CRD object's existence causes the apiextensions-apiserver to dynamically provision a new REST endpoint for the custom resource kind it describes.

The custom resource *instances* (CR data) live entirely as `unstructured.Unstructured` throughout their server-side lifecycle. There are no compiled-in Go structs for user-defined CRs. The `crdHandler` HTTP layer reads the CRD spec (via lister → informer → typed clientset), constructs a schema validator and storage, and routes all CRUD operations through `customresource/etcd.go`, which stores and retrieves raw JSON documents as `Unstructured` maps.

### The Role of Scheme and GVK in Type Registration

`runtime.Scheme` is the central registry. When `install.Install(scheme)` is called at apiserver startup, it registers three versions of `CustomResourceDefinition`:
- The internal hub type under `apiextensions.k8s.io/__internal`
- The v1 external type under `apiextensions.k8s.io/v1`
- The v1beta1 external type under `apiextensions.k8s.io/v1beta1`

The Scheme's `gvkToType` and `typeToGVK` maps enable the codec factory to decode incoming API requests (regardless of version) into the hub type for validation and storage, and to encode outgoing responses into the client's preferred version. Version priority (`v1 > v1beta1`) is also stored in the Scheme and governs which version the API server advertises as preferred.

For *custom resource instances*, no Scheme registration occurs (there are no Go structs). Instead, the `crdHandler` constructs a runtime codec that directly serializes/deserializes JSON into `Unstructured` maps, bypassing the Scheme for the data plane.

### How Unstructured Enables Dynamic Custom Resource Access

`unstructured.Unstructured` implements both `runtime.Object` (so it can flow through the generic apiserver machinery) and `metav1.Object` (so standard metadata accessors like `GetName()`, `GetUID()`, `GetLabels()` work correctly). Its backing store is a plain `map[string]interface{}`, which can represent any JSON document without needing a compiled Go type.

The `DynamicClient` in `client-go/dynamic` exploits this: it takes a `schema.GroupVersionResource` at runtime, constructs the URL path, and returns `*unstructured.Unstructured` for all operations. This makes it the primary API for controllers that manage arbitrary CRs (e.g., operator frameworks, GitOps controllers). The `dynamicSharedInformerFactory` builds on top of this, providing a watch-based cache keyed by GVR.

### The Checkpoint Between Server-Side Storage and Client-Side Informer Caching

The critical checkpoint is the **List/Watch API**. When a `CustomResourceDefinitionInformer` starts, it calls:
1. `client.ApiextensionsV1().CustomResourceDefinitions().List(...)` — initial sync, populates the local `cache.Indexer`
2. `client.ApiextensionsV1().CustomResourceDefinitions().Watch(...)` — ongoing watch stream, incrementally updates the Indexer

The `crdHandler` server-side also uses a `CustomResourceDefinitionLister` (backed by its own informer) to read established CRDs without round-tripping to etcd on every request. This means there are two informer caches: one inside the apiextensions-apiserver process (server-side, for routing), and one in any external controller process (client-side, for reconciliation). Both subscribe to the same etcd-backed watch stream.

---

## Summary

A Custom Resource Definition flows through the Kubernetes ecosystem in two distinct planes: the **CRD meta-object plane** (the CRD itself, persisted as a typed Go struct through `genericregistry.Store` backed by etcd) and the **CR instance plane** (actual custom resource data, handled entirely as `unstructured.Unstructured` with no compiled-in Go types). `apimachinery` provides the universal vocabulary—`Scheme`, `GVK/GVR`, `TypeMeta/ObjectMeta`, and `Unstructured`—that all four sub-projects share; `apiextensions-apiserver` implements the CRD type definitions, validation, storage, and dynamic HTTP routing; and `client-go` provides both the generated typed clients/informers/listers for the CRD meta-objects and the generic `DynamicClient` + `dynamicSharedInformerFactory` that let controllers manage arbitrary custom resource instances by GVR at runtime.

---

1. **Foundation — apimachinery**
   - `schema.GroupVersionKind` / `GroupVersionResource` — the universal address space for all Kubernetes types
   - `runtime.Scheme` — bidirectional registry mapping Go types ↔ GVKs; central to encoding/decoding
   - `metav1.TypeMeta` + `metav1.ObjectMeta` — embedded in every persisted API object
   - `unstructured.Unstructured` — `map[string]interface{}` representation that can hold any API object without a registered Go struct

2. **Type Definitions — apiextensions-apiserver**
   - Internal hub type (`apiextensions.CustomResourceDefinition`) registered with `SchemeGroupVersion = apiextensions.k8s.io/__internal`

3. **Server-Side — validation → etcd storage → HTTP handler**
   - **Validation**: `apiserver/validation.SchemaValidator` validates CR instances against the CRD's OpenAPI schema before admission
   - **CRD etcd storage** (`registry/customresourcedefinition/etcd.go`): Generic store backed by etcd via `genericregistry.Store`; persists `CustomResourceDefinition` meta-objects; adds cleanup finalizer on delete
   - **CR etcd storage** (`registry/customresource/etcd.go`): Separate generic store for actual custom resource instances; `NewFunc` returns `&unstructured.Unstructured{}` pre-tagged with GVK; `ScaleREST` projects replica paths into `autoscaling/v1.Scale`

   - **SharedIndexInformer** (`client/informers/.../customresourcedefinition.go`): Uses the typed clientset's List/Watch to populate an in-memory `cache.Indexer`; shared across all consumers to minimise API server load

   - **DynamicClient** (`client-go/dynamic/simple.go`): GVR-addressed client that sends/receives raw JSON; all objects as `*unstructured.Unstructured`; used by controllers that manage arbitrary CRs without compiled-in type knowledge
   - **Dynamic informer** (`client-go/dynamic/dynamicinformer/`): Like typed informers but keyed by `schema.GroupVersionResource`; enables watching arbitrary CRs generically

---

## Analysis

### Cross-Project Dependency Flow

```
apimachinery
  ├── runtime.Scheme         ←── used by ALL other sub-projects
  ├── schema.GVK / GVR       ←── universal type address
  ├── metav1.TypeMeta/ObjectMeta ←── embedded in every API object
  └── unstructured.Unstructured  ←── dynamic CR representation
         ↓
apiextensions-apiserver
  ├── types.go (hub + v1)    ←── CRD Go structs, embed apimachinery types
  ├── register.go / install  ←── Scheme registration
  ├── validation             ←── structural schema, CEL, OpenAPI validation
  ├── registry/crd/etcd.go   ←── CRD meta-object persistence in etcd
  ├── registry/cr/etcd.go    ←── CR instance persistence via Unstructured
  └── apiserver/handler.go   ←── dynamic HTTP routing, uses listers
         ↓
client-go
  ├── dynamic.Interface      ←── GVR-addressed, Unstructured-based client
  ├── dynamic/informer       ←── generic informer for any GVR
  └── tools/cache            ←── SharedIndexInformer, Indexer (shared infra)
         ↑
apiextensions-apiserver (generated clients)
  ├── clientset              ←── typed client wrapping client-go/rest
  ├── informers (generated)  ←── typed SharedIndexInformer
  └── listers (generated)    ←── typed read from Indexer
```

### How CRD Types Bridge the Four Sub-Projects

The `CustomResourceDefinition` object itself follows the universal Kubernetes object pattern established by `apimachinery`: it embeds `metav1.TypeMeta` and `metav1.ObjectMeta` from `apimachinery`, is registered in `runtime.Scheme` (also from `apimachinery`), and is persisted through `genericregistry.Store`. This means a CRD object is handled identically to a Pod or Deployment at the storage level—the only difference is its *effect*: a CRD object's existence causes the apiextensions-apiserver to dynamically provision a new REST endpoint for the custom resource kind it describes.

The custom resource *instances* (CR data) live entirely as `unstructured.Unstructured` throughout their server-side lifecycle. There are no compiled-in Go structs for user-defined CRs. The `crdHandler` HTTP layer reads the CRD spec (via lister → informer → typed clientset), constructs a schema validator and storage, and routes all CRUD operations through `customresource/etcd.go`, which stores and retrieves raw JSON documents as `Unstructured` maps.

### The Role of Scheme and GVK in Type Registration

`runtime.Scheme` is the central registry. When `install.Install(scheme)` is called at apiserver startup, it registers three versions of `CustomResourceDefinition`:
- The internal hub type under `apiextensions.k8s.io/__internal`
- The v1 external type under `apiextensions.k8s.io/v1`
- The v1beta1 external type under `apiextensions.k8s.io/v1beta1`

The Scheme's `gvkToType` and `typeToGVK` maps enable the codec factory to decode incoming API requests (regardless of version) into the hub type for validation and storage, and to encode outgoing responses into the client's preferred version. Version priority (`v1 > v1beta1`) is also stored in the Scheme and governs which version the API server advertises as preferred.

For *custom resource instances*, no Scheme registration occurs (there are no Go structs). Instead, the `crdHandler` constructs a runtime codec that directly serializes/deserializes JSON into `Unstructured` maps, bypassing the Scheme for the data plane.

### How Unstructured Enables Dynamic Custom Resource Access

`unstructured.Unstructured` implements both `runtime.Object` (so it can flow through the generic apiserver machinery) and `metav1.Object` (so standard metadata accessors like `GetName()`, `GetUID()`, `GetLabels()` work correctly). Its backing store is a plain `map[string]interface{}`, which can represent any JSON document without needing a compiled Go type.

The `DynamicClient` in `client-go/dynamic` exploits this: it takes a `schema.GroupVersionResource` at runtime, constructs the URL path, and returns `*unstructured.Unstructured` for all operations. This makes it the primary API for controllers that manage arbitrary CRs (e.g., operator frameworks, GitOps controllers). The `dynamicSharedInformerFactory` builds on top of this, providing a watch-based cache keyed by GVR.

### The Checkpoint Between Server-Side Storage and Client-Side Informer Caching

The critical checkpoint is the **List/Watch API**. When a `CustomResourceDefinitionInformer` starts, it calls:
1. `client.ApiextensionsV1().CustomResourceDefinitions().List(...)` — initial sync, populates the local `cache.Indexer`
2. `client.ApiextensionsV1().CustomResourceDefinitions().Watch(...)` — ongoing watch stream, incrementally updates the Indexer

The `crdHandler` server-side also uses a `CustomResourceDefinitionLister` (backed by its own informer) to read established CRDs without round-tripping to etcd on every request. This means there are two informer caches: one inside the apiextensions-apiserver process (server-side, for routing), and one in any external controller process (client-side, for reconciliation). Both subscribe to the same etcd-backed watch stream.

---

## Summary

A Custom Resource Definition flows through the Kubernetes ecosystem in two distinct planes: the **CRD meta-object plane** (the CRD itself, persisted as a typed Go struct through `genericregistry.Store` backed by etcd) and the **CR instance plane** (actual custom resource data, handled entirely as `unstructured.Unstructured` with no compiled-in Go types). `apimachinery` provides the universal vocabulary—`Scheme`, `GVK/GVR`, `TypeMeta/ObjectMeta`, and `Unstructured`—that all four sub-projects share; `apiextensions-apiserver` implements the CRD type definitions, validation, storage, and dynamic HTTP routing; and `client-go` provides both the generated typed clients/informers/listers for the CRD meta-objects and the generic `DynamicClient` + `dynamicSharedInformerFactory` that let controllers manage arbitrary custom resource instances by GVR at runtime.

### Cross-Project Dependency Flow

```

  ├── schema.GVK / GVR          ← universal type address, used everywhere
  ├── metav1.TypeMeta/ObjectMeta← embedded in every API object
  └── unstructured.Unstructured ← dynamic CR representation, core of CR data plane
         |
         v
apiextensions-apiserver
  ├── types.go (hub + v1)       ← CRD Go structs, embed apimachinery types
  ├── register.go / install     ← Scheme registration (AddToScheme, SetVersionPriority)
  ├── apiserver/validation      ← structural schema, CEL, OpenAPI validation
  ├── registry/crd/etcd.go      ← CRD meta-object persistence in etcd
  ├── registry/cr/etcd.go       ← CR instance persistence via Unstructured
  └── apiserver/handler.go      ← dynamic HTTP routing; reads CRDs via listers
         |
         v (uses client-go/rest and cache infrastructure)
client-go
  ├── dynamic.Interface         ← GVR-addressed, Unstructured-based CRUD
  ├── dynamic/informer          ← generic informer for any GVR
  └── tools/cache               ← SharedIndexInformer, Indexer (shared infrastructure)

apiextensions-apiserver (generated client-side)
  ├── clientset                 ← typed client wrapping client-go/rest
  ├── informers (generated)     ← typed SharedIndexInformer for CRD objects

```

### How CRD Types Bridge the Four Sub-Projects

The `CustomResourceDefinition` object follows the universal pattern established by `apimachinery`: it embeds `metav1.TypeMeta` and `metav1.ObjectMeta`, is registered in `runtime.Scheme`, and is persisted through `genericregistry.Store`. The CRD object is thus handled identically to a Pod or Deployment at the storage level—the only difference is its *effect*: a CRD object's existence causes the apiextensions-apiserver's `crdHandler` to dynamically provision a new REST endpoint group for the custom resource kind it describes.

Custom resource *instances* (CR data) live entirely as `unstructured.Unstructured` throughout their server-side lifecycle. There are no compiled-in Go structs for user-defined types. The `crdHandler` reads the CRD spec via a `CustomResourceDefinitionLister` (itself backed by a generated informer and typed clientset), constructs a schema validator and `customResourceStorage`, and routes all CRUD operations through `customresource/etcd.go`, which stores and retrieves raw JSON as `Unstructured` maps.

`runtime.Scheme` is the central registry. When `install.Install(scheme)` is called at apiserver startup:
- The internal hub type is registered under `apiextensions.k8s.io/__internal`
- The v1 external type under `apiextensions.k8s.io/v1`
- The v1beta1 external type under `apiextensions.k8s.io/v1beta1`
- Version priority is set: v1 > v1beta1

The Scheme's `gvkToType` and `typeToGVK` maps enable the codec factory to decode incoming API requests (regardless of version) into the hub type for validation and storage, and to encode outgoing responses into the client's requested version.

For *custom resource instances*, no Scheme registration occurs. Instead, `crdHandler` builds a runtime codec that directly serializes/deserializes JSON into `Unstructured` maps, bypassing the Scheme for the CR data plane. The GVK is carried inside the Unstructured's `Object["apiVersion"]` and `Object["kind"]` fields.

`unstructured.Unstructured` implements both `runtime.Object` (allowing it to flow through the generic apiserver machinery unchanged) and `metav1.Object` (so standard metadata accessors like `GetName()`, `GetUID()`, `GetLabels()` work correctly via nested map traversal). Its backing store is a `map[string]interface{}` that can represent any JSON document.

The `DynamicClient` exploits this: it takes a `schema.GroupVersionResource` at runtime, constructs the URL path, serializes/deserializes JSON, and returns `*unstructured.Unstructured`. This makes it the primary API for controllers that manage arbitrary CRs without advance knowledge of the schema. The `dynamicSharedInformerFactory` builds on top, providing a watch-based cache keyed by GVR so controllers can avoid repeated API server calls.

### The Checkpoint Between Server-Side Storage and Client-Side Informer Caching

The critical bridge is the **List/Watch API endpoint**. When a `CustomResourceDefinitionInformer` starts, it executes:
1. `client.ApiextensionsV1().CustomResourceDefinitions().List(...)` — performs an initial bulk sync, populating the local `cache.Indexer` with all current CRDs
2. `client.ApiextensionsV1().CustomResourceDefinitions().Watch(...)` — subscribes to an etcd watch stream; incremental Add/Update/Delete events keep the Indexer current

The `crdHandler` server-side also holds its own `CustomResourceDefinitionLister` (backed by an internal informer) to look up established CRDs on every request without hitting etcd directly. This means there are two independent informer caches: one inside the apiextensions-apiserver process (for HTTP routing decisions) and one in any external controller process (for reconciliation logic). Both subscribe to the same underlying etcd watch stream through the API server's watch-cache layer.

---

A Custom Resource Definition flows through the Kubernetes ecosystem across two planes: the **CRD meta-object plane**, where the CRD itself is a typed Go struct persisted through `genericregistry.Store` into etcd and accessed via generated clientsets, informers, and listers; and the **CR instance plane**, where actual custom resource data is handled entirely as `unstructured.Unstructured` with no compiled-in Go types, routed dynamically by `crdHandler` using GVK/GVR from apimachinery's schema package. `apimachinery` provides the universal vocabulary—`Scheme`, `GVK/GVR`, `TypeMeta/ObjectMeta`, and `Unstructured`—that all four sub-projects depend on; `apiextensions-apiserver` implements the CRD type definitions, validation pipeline, dual etcd storage layers (one for CRD meta-objects, one for CR instances), and dynamic HTTP routing; and `client-go` supplies both the generated typed clients/informers/listers for managing CRD objects and the generic `DynamicClient`/`dynamicSharedInformerFactory` that let controllers watch and reconcile arbitrary custom resource instances by GVR at runtime.

The `DynamicClient` exploits this: it takes a `schema.GroupVersionResource` at runtime, constructs the URL path, serializes/deserializes JSON, and returns `*unstructured.Unstructured`. This makes it the primary API for controllers that manage arbitrary CRs without advance knowledge of the schema. The `dynamicSharedInformerFactory` builds on top, providing a watch-based cache keyed by GVR so controllers can avoid repeated API server calls.

### The Checkpoint Between Server-Side Storage and Client-Side Informer Caching

The critical bridge is the **List/Watch API endpoint**. When a `CustomResourceDefinitionInformer` starts, it executes:
1. `client.ApiextensionsV1().CustomResourceDefinitions().List(...)` — performs an initial bulk sync, populating the local `cache.Indexer` with all current CRDs
2. `client.ApiextensionsV1().CustomResourceDefinitions().Watch(...)` — subscribes to an etcd watch stream; incremental Add/Update/Delete events keep the Indexer current

The `crdHandler` server-side also holds its own `CustomResourceDefinitionLister` (backed by an internal informer) to look up established CRDs on every request without hitting etcd directly. This means there are two independent informer caches: one inside the apiextensions-apiserver process (for HTTP routing decisions) and one in any external controller process (for reconciliation logic). Both subscribe to the same underlying etcd watch stream through the API server's watch-cache layer.

---

## Summary

A Custom Resource Definition flows through the Kubernetes ecosystem across two planes: the **CRD meta-object plane**, where the CRD itself is a typed Go struct persisted through `genericregistry.Store` into etcd and accessed via generated clientsets, informers, and listers; and the **CR instance plane**, where actual custom resource data is handled entirely as `unstructured.Unstructured` with no compiled-in Go types, routed dynamically by `crdHandler` using GVK/GVR from apimachinery's schema package. `apimachinery` provides the universal vocabulary—`Scheme`, `GVK/GVR`, `TypeMeta/ObjectMeta`, and `Unstructured`—that all four sub-projects depend on; `apiextensions-apiserver` implements the CRD type definitions, validation pipeline, dual etcd storage layers (one for CRD meta-objects, one for CR instances), and dynamic HTTP routing; and `client-go` supplies both the generated typed clients/informers/listers for managing CRD objects and the generic `DynamicClient`/`dynamicSharedInformerFactory` that let controllers watch and reconcile arbitrary custom resource instances by GVR at runtime.
